mechanism_narrative: A persistently low or falling federal funds rate lowers the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, mechanically raising present-value valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days, independent of any triggering threshold.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 1.98% is well below historical median, consistent with an accommodative policy stance
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and 20-day forward SPX return stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or returns are not significantly higher when FEDFUNDS is below its 5-year median (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises more than 0.3 percentage points over a rolling 3-month window, it signals deteriorating labor market momentum that precedes earnings downgrades, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.3pp over trailing 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE currently at 5.5, a level historically associated with labor-market softening
failure_condition: Triggered instances show positive median 60-day forward returns in more than 55% of cases, or Rank IC falls below 0.03 conditioned on the trigger over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A high nominal federal funds rate combined with decelerating CPI inflation implies a rising real policy rate that tightens financial conditions faster than headline rates suggest, jointly producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days; neither series alone captures this effect.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 1.98% alongside CPIAUCSL level of 215.132 defines the current real-rate proxy
failure_condition: The interaction term (FEDFUNDS minus CPI YoY change proxy) shows Rank IC below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or the single-series FEDFUNDS model matches or outperforms the joint model out-of-sample.

mechanism_narrative: During an industrial expansion regime, rising INDPRO signals strengthening corporate output and margins that support forward equity returns; the same rate of INDPRO increase during a contraction regime instead reflects late-cycle overheating and predicts negative returns, so the direction of the effect flips with the regime.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 111.18 with GDPC1 at 11701.9 places the economy in a moderate growth phase
regime_hint: industrial expansion phase distinguished from late-cycle contraction
failure_condition: Direction of the INDPRO-return relationship fails to reverse sign between expansion and contraction sub-samples, or Rank IC is below 0.03 in the expansion regime specifically over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in real retail sales (RSAFS) reflects resilient consumer demand that feeds directly into near-term corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days on an always-on basis.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 385448 million dollars reflects the latest consumer spending print
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS momentum and 5-day forward SPX return remains below 0.02 over any rolling 1-year window, or sign concordance falls below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production greater than 1% signals an abrupt supply-side contraction that raises recession probability and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month as markets reprice growth expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO level of 111.1807 provides the base for computing month-over-month deviations
failure_condition: In triggered instances, median 20-day forward SPX return is non-negative in more than 50% of occurrences, or fewer than 5 trigger events occur across the historical sample making the estimate unreliable.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll growth (PAYEMS) combined with a simultaneously low unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a tight labor market approaching capacity constraints, jointly predicting negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter as wage-cost pressure threatens margins, an effect not visible from either series alone.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 137754 thousand alongside UNRATE at 5.5 percent defines current labor tightness
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS-UNRATE interaction model fails to outperform a single-series UNRATE-only baseline in out-of-sample Rank IC over any rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening regime (FEDFUNDS rising), a sharp contraction in housing starts (HOUST) amplifies the negative demand shock transmitted from higher mortgage rates, producing outsized negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days; in an easing regime the same HOUST decline has muted or no predictive power.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1032 thousand units combined with FEDFUNDS at 1.98% describes current housing-rate conditions
failure_condition: The negative HOUST-return relationship fails to be materially stronger (by Rank IC) during tightening sub-periods versus easing sub-periods, or overall Rank IC stays below 0.03 across rolling 2-year windows.
